message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): connection-refused transport error contract (tick 320)

Added `distributed_http_client_connection_refused_returns_err` — pins
that `DistributedHttpClient::get` returns `Err` promptly when the target
port has no listener.

Completes a three-outcome matrix for the HTTP client return type:
- 2xx body payload            → Ok(Response{status: 2xx, body})
- non-2xx response            → Ok(Response{status: 4xx/5xx, body})   (tick 319)
- connection failed to open   → Err(...)                              (this tick)

Callers rely on this separation to route retries correctly: transport
`Err` is retryable (peer may come back), non-2xx `Ok` may or may not be
retryable depending on the status code.

Test bind-then-drops a loopback port to deterministically secure a
port-number that has no listener, then asserts the client returns `Err`
in under 5 seconds (the contract is "does not hang" — kernel connect
refusal on loopback is near-instant so the bound is generous but any
regression that changed connect behavior to hang would still fire).

Test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 15s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
